column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-async-data (async-01 … async-10).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Structural rules that
# grep cannot express (Task-in-onAppear containment, AsyncImage single-arg shape) live in ast-grep/*.yml.
# ABSENCE defects (async-02 missing loading, async-04 missing empty, async-08 missing .refreshable) CANNOT
# be grepped — they are DETECT-only (see SKILL.md). The positive proxies below locate the load site to read.

column4: bare unstructured Task in a view — if it launches view-lifecycle async load, it is NOT cancelled on disappear → .task / .task(id:) (lifecycle owner; cross_ref concurrency-safety for isolation)
swallowed async error — the throw is dropped and no error state is surfaced to the user; confirm an error path renders (async-03)
raw URLSession in a view layer — confirm the decode runs OFF the main actor and the @MainActor write is a checkable hop, not on-main blocking (async-05; cross_ref concurrency-safety)
search field — if its query drives a fetch, confirm the query is DEBOUNCED before firing (async-06), else a request per keystroke
AsyncImage — confirm the error/empty phase is handled (not just url+placeholder) and a cache exists for lists/grids (async-07)
.refreshable present — GOOD; READ to confirm its action re-runs the same loader the .task uses (async-08 is the MISSING case, not this)
.redacted present — GOOD skeleton; confirm reason is .placeholder and it is driven by the loading flag (async-10 is the MISSING case)
